text: Microscope Memory is a zoom-based hierarchical memory system built in pure Rust
The system uses 9 depth levels (D0-D8), like a microscope with increasing magnification
All blocks are uniform size (256 chars). Only the zoom level determines what you see
Binary format uses mmap for zero-copy reads — sub-microsecond query performance
SHA-256 hash chain and Merkle tree provide cryptographic integrity verification
The tiered spatial index gives 17x speedup over linear scanning
Deterministic 3D coordinates: same content always maps to the same spatial position
Store operation appends to a log file and extends the hash chain in real-time
Rebuild merges the append log back into the main index with full crypto rebuild
The CPU cache analogy: L1/L2/L3 hierarchy applied to cognitive memory
